D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

FDM Multi-Filament Gradient

FDM cannot really blend two colours in the nozzle — it swaps filaments between thin layers, and the eye reads the stripes as a gradient

A multi-material FDM (Prusa MMU, Mosaic Palette) does not blend colour. It swaps spools — purging the old filament out into a sacrificial purge tower beside the part, then loading the new one. To fake a gradient it alternates thin layers in a varying ratio; close up you still see distinct stripes, but from a few steps back they fuse into a smooth transition.

Tune the stripe pitch and watch the cross-section's bands coarsen or tighten. Coarse stripes look obviously banded; finer stripes blend in the eye but increase the purge tower height because there are more filament swaps to do.
